passages.title: Ways to Travel
passage: There are many ways to travel.
Do you like to travel by land?
Use buses or trains.
They move across land.
Do you like to travel by water?
Use ferries or boats.
They move across water.
There is an awesome way to travel.
That's using airplanes or helicopters.
They fly through the air.
Which way do you want to travel?

passages.title: We're on Time!
passage: Rosa's family is in the taxi.
"We're going to be late!" says Rosa.
Too many cars are on the road.
"Let's ride a bus!" says Mom.
"We're going to be late!" says Rosa.
Too many people are at the bus stop.
"Let's ride a bike!" says Mom.
"We're going to be late!" says Rosa.
"Don't worry," says Dad.
"Hooray!" shouts Rosa. "We are on time!"
Rosa's family gets on the plane!

questions.question: Which word means "to go from one place to another"?
questions.explanationKo: travel은 한 장소에서 다른 장소로 가다는 뜻입니다.
questions.hintKo: 한 장소에서 다른 장소로 가다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "a locomotive that pulls a long connected group of railroad cars"?
questions.explanationKo: train은 철길 위를 달리는 기차를 뜻합니다.

questions.question: Which word means "from one side to the other side of something"?
questions.explanationKo: across는 한쪽에서 다른 쪽으로 지나가다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "a ship that carries people, vehicles and goods across water"?
questions.explanationKo: ferry는 사람이나 물건을 물 건너편으로 나르는 배입니다.
questions.hintKo: 사람이나 물건을 물 건너편으로 나르는 배입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "impressive or incredible action that was unbelievable"?
Which word means "a group of people made of a father, mother, and children"?
Which word means "arriving or happening after the expected time"?
Which word means "a hard surface built for vehicles to travel on"?
Which word means "to say something very loudly or angrily"?
Which word means "to get into a bus, plane, train, etc."?
questions.explanationKo: get on은 버스나 비행기 등에 타다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: What is the reading about?
questions.explanationKo: 본문은 여러 가지 이동 방법에 대해 설명합니다.
questions.evidence: There are many ways to travel.

questions.question: What can you use to travel by land?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 땅으로 이동할 때 버스나 기차를 이용한다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: Use buses or trains.

questions.question: Where do buses and trains move?
questions.evidence: They move across land.

questions.question: What can you use to travel by water?
questions.explanationKo: 물 위로 이동할 때는 페리나 보트를 이용합니다.
questions.evidence: Use ferries or boats.

questions.question: Where do ferries and boats move?
questions.evidence: They move across water.

questions.question: What is an awesome way to travel?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 비행기나 헬리콥터를 이용하는 것이 멋진 여행 방법이라고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: That's using airplanes or helicopters.

questions.question: What flies through the air?
questions.evidence: They fly through the air.

questions.question: If you want to cross water, which transportation should you choose?
questions.explanationKo: 물을 건너갈 때는 본문에 나온 ferries or boats가 알맞습니다.
questions.evidence: Use ferries or boats. They move across water.

questions.question: Which group travels through the air?
questions.explanationKo: through the air는 하늘을 날아 이동한다는 뜻이므로 비행기와 헬리콥터가 맞습니다.
questions.evidence: They fly through the air.

questions.question: What question does the passage ask at the end?
choices: Which way do you want to travel?
questions.explanationKo: 본문 마지막에서 어떤 방법으로 여행하고 싶은지 묻습니다.
questions.evidence: Which way do you want to travel?
choiceMeanings: Which way do you want to travel?

questions.question: Where is Rosa's family at first?
questions.explanationKo: 처음에 Rosa의 가족은 택시 안에 있습니다.
questions.evidence: Rosa's family is in the taxi.

questions.question: What does Rosa say again and again?
choices: We are going to be late!
questions.explanationKo: Rosa는 반복해서 늦을 것 같다고 말합니다.
questions.evidence: "We're going to be late!" says Rosa.

questions.question: What is on the road?
questions.evidence: Too many cars are on the road.

questions.question: What does Mom say after seeing many cars on the road?
questions.evidence: "Let's ride a bus!" says Mom.

questions.question: What is at the bus stop?
questions.evidence: Too many people are at the bus stop.

questions.question: What does Dad say to Rosa?
questions.evidence: "Don't worry," says Dad.

questions.question: Where does Rosa's family get on at the end?
questions.evidence: Rosa's family gets on the plane!

questions.question: What transportation does Rosa's family try after the taxi road is crowded?
questions.explanationKo: 도로에 차가 많자 엄마는 버스를 타자고 합니다.
questions.evidence: Too many cars are on the road. "Let's ride a bus!" says Mom.

questions.question: Why can't the family easily take the bus?
choices: Too many people are at the bus stop.
The bus is in the water.
questions.explanationKo: 버스 정류장에 사람이 너무 많아서 버스를 쉽게 탈 수 없습니다.
questions.evidence: Too many people are at the bus stop.
choiceMeanings: 그 버스 is in 그 물

questions.question: What does "We are on time" mean in the story?
choices: They are still in the taxi.
questions.evidence: "Hooray!" shouts Rosa. "We are on time!"
choiceMeanings: 그들은 are still in 그 택시

passages.title: Wind Blows!
passage: Wind is moving air.
We can't see the wind.
But we can see what the wind does.
Wind makes flags flutter.
Wind holds kites in the sky.
Wind shakes leaves on trees.
Strong wind turns an umbrella inside out.
Very fast wind can lift a roof off a house!
What else does the wind do?

passages.title: Fisherman and Wind
passage: Wind blows too strong.
Fisherman cannot fish.
He goes to see Wind.
He puts Wind in a hole.
Now, Wind doesn't blow.
Fisherman catches many fish.
Days go by.
Bad things happen.
The weather is too hot.
The fish begin to die.
Fisherman goes to see Wind.
Wind promises to make gentle wind.
Fisherman takes Wind out of the hole.
But Wind forgets his promise.
So sometimes, we feel strong wind.

questions.question: Which word means "to move lightly and quickly as when a bird or insect moves its wings"?
questions.explanationKo: flutter는 새나 곤충의 날개처럼 가볍고 빠르게 움직인다는 뜻입니다.
questions.hintKo: 새나 곤충의 날개처럼 가볍고 빠르게 움직인다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "to move something quickly back and forth or up and down with short, quick movements"?
questions.explanationKo: shake는 짧고 빠르게 앞뒤나 위아래로 흔든다는 뜻입니다.
questions.hintKo: 짧고 빠르게 앞뒤나 위아래로 흔든다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "to keep something in a particular position or place"?
questions.explanationKo: hold는 어떤 것을 특정한 위치나 장소에 있게 한다는 뜻입니다.
questions.hintKo: 어떤 것을 특정한 위치나 장소에 있게 한다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "a toy made of a light frame covered with paper or cloth that flies in the wind"?
questions.explanationKo: kite는 바람에 날리는 가벼운 장난감인 연을 뜻합니다.
questions.hintKo: 바람에 날리는 가벼운 장난감인 연을 뜻합니다.

questions.question: Which word means "to move something or somebody upward to a higher position"?
questions.explanationKo: lift는 무언가를 위로 들어 올리다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "a person who catches fish for sport or a living"?
questions.explanationKo: fisherman은 물고기를 잡는 사람, 즉 어부를 뜻합니다.
questions.hintKo: 물고기를 잡는 사람, 즉 어부를 뜻합니다.

questions.question: Which word means "the temperature and other outside conditions such as sun, rain, wind, and clouds"?
questions.explanationKo: weather는 햇빛, 비, 바람, 구름 등 바깥의 상태인 날씨를 뜻합니다.
questions.hintKo: 햇빛, 비, 바람, 구름 등 바깥의 상태인 날씨를 뜻합니다.

questions.question: Which word means "to tell somebody that you will definitely do or not do something"?
questions.explanationKo: promise는 어떤 일을 꼭 하겠다고 말하는 것, 즉 약속하다는 뜻입니다.
questions.hintKo: 어떤 일을 꼭 하겠다고 말하는 것, 즉 약속하다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "calm, kind and careful in the way you do things"?
questions.explanationKo: gentle은 차분하고 부드럽거나 세지 않다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "to not remember information from the past"?
questions.explanationKo: forget은 과거의 정보를 기억하지 못하다, 즉 잊다는 뜻입니다.
questions.hintKo: 과거의 정보를 기억하지 못하다, 즉 잊다는 뜻입니다.

questions.question: What is wind?
questions.evidence: Wind is moving air.

questions.question: Can we see the wind?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에 우리는 바람을 볼 수 없다고 나옵니다.
questions.evidence: We can't see the wind.

questions.question: What does wind make flags do?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 바람은 깃발을 펄럭이게 한다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: Wind makes flags flutter.

questions.question: What does wind hold in the sky?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 바람은 연을 하늘에 떠 있게 한다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: Wind holds kites in the sky.

questions.question: What does wind shake on trees?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 바람은 나무의 잎을 흔든다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: Wind shakes leaves on trees.

questions.question: What can strong wind do to an umbrella?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 강한 바람은 우산을 뒤집을 수 있다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: Strong wind turns an umbrella inside out.

questions.question: What can very fast wind lift off a house?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 매우 빠른 바람은 집의 지붕을 날려 보낼 수 있다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: Very fast wind can lift a roof off a house!

questions.question: How do we know the wind is there if we can't see it?
choices: We see what the wind does.
questions.explanationKo: 바람 자체는 볼 수 없지만, 바람이 하는 일을 보고 알 수 있습니다.
questions.evidence: But we can see what the wind does.
choiceMeanings: 우리는 see what 그 바람 does

questions.question: Which is an example of what wind can do?
questions.explanationKo: 본문의 예시 중 바람은 깃발을 펄럭이게 합니다.
questions.evidence: Wind makes flags flutter.

questions.question: What may happen when the wind is very fast?
choices: A roof can lift off a house.
questions.explanationKo: 매우 빠른 바람이 불면 집의 지붕이 날아갈 수 있습니다.
questions.evidence: Very fast wind can lift a roof off a house!

questions.question: Why can the fisherman NOT fish?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 바람이 너무 강해서 어부가 물고기를 잡을 수 없다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: Wind blows too strong. Fisherman cannot fish.

questions.question: Where does the fisherman put Wind?
questions.evidence: He puts Wind in a hole.

questions.question: What happens when Wind doesn't blow?
questions.explanationKo: 바람이 불지 않자 어부는 물고기를 많이 잡았습니다.
questions.evidence: Now, Wind doesn't blow. Fisherman catches many fish.

questions.question: What is the weather like after days go by?
questions.evidence: The weather is too hot.

questions.question: What begins to die?
questions.evidence: The fish begin to die.

questions.question: What does Wind promise to make?
questions.evidence: Wind promises to make gentle wind.

questions.question: What does Wind forget?
questions.evidence: But Wind forgets his promise.

questions.question: Why does the fisherman put Wind in a hole?
questions.explanationKo: 어부는 바람이 너무 강해서 물고기를 잡지 못했습니다. Wind를 구멍에 넣자 바람이 멈추고 물고기를 많이 잡았습니다.
questions.evidence: He puts Wind in a hole. Now, Wind doesn't blow. Fisherman catches many fish.

questions.question: What happens after the fisherman takes Wind out of the hole?
questions.explanationKo: 어부가 Wind를 구멍에서 꺼낸 뒤, Wind는 자신의 약속을 잊었습니다.
questions.evidence: Fisherman takes Wind out of the hole. But Wind forgets his promise.

questions.question: What can we learn from the story?
questions.explanationKo: 이야기에서 Wind가 약속을 잊어서 다시 강한 바람이 불게 되므로, 약속을 지키는 것이 중요하다는 점을 알 수 있습니다.
questions.evidence: Wind promises to make gentle wind. But Wind forgets his promise.

passages.title: Insects Talk Too!
passage: People talk to each other.
Insects talk to each other, too.
Insects talk about where food is.
They talk to give warnings about enemies.
But they do not talk like people.
They have their own way of talking.
Ants touch other ants.
Crickets make sounds.
Fireflies flash light.
The next time you see an insect, watch and listen.

passages.title: Ants Live in Groups
passage: Ants live in groups.
There are four kinds of ants in their group.
They are a queen, workers, soldiers, and males.
There is only one queen in a group.
The queen lays eggs.
Workers are the queen's daughters.
They build houses and gather food.
Soldiers are daughters, too.
They are big and strong.
They defend houses and fight enemies.
Males mate with the queen to produce eggs.

questions.question: Which word means "a very small living thing with six legs"?
Which word means "something that tells people about possible danger"?
Which word means "a person or thing that wants to hurt another"?
Which word means "to put your hand or body against something"?
Which word means "something that you can hear"?
Which word means "a woman ruler or the female insect that lays eggs"?
Which word means "a member that fights or protects a group"?
Which word means "a person’s female child"?
Which word means "to bring things together"?
Which word means "to protect from danger"?
What do insects do with each other?
questions.evidence: Insects talk to each other, too.

questions.question: What do insects talk about?
questions.evidence: Insects talk about where food is.

questions.question: What do insects give warnings about?
questions.evidence: They talk to give warnings about enemies.

questions.question: Do insects talk like people?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 곤충은 사람처럼 말하지 않는다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: But they do not talk like people.

questions.question: How do ants talk to other ants?
questions.evidence: Ants touch other ants.

questions.question: What do crickets make?
questions.evidence: Crickets make sounds.

questions.question: What do fireflies flash?
questions.evidence: Fireflies flash light.

questions.question: What is a different way to ask “Insects talk, too”?
questions.explanationKo: 본문의 “Insects talk to each other, too.”를 질문형으로 바꾼 문제입니다.
questions.evidence: Insects talk to each other, too.

questions.question: Which insect uses light to communicate?
questions.explanationKo: 본문의 핵심 개념은 곤충마다 다른 방식으로 이야기한다는 점입니다.
questions.evidence: Fireflies flash light.

questions.question: What should you do the next time you see an insect?
questions.explanationKo: 마지막 문장에서 곤충을 보면 보고 들으라고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: The next time you see an insect, watch and listen.

questions.question: Where do ants live?
questions.evidence: Ants live in groups.

questions.question: How many kinds of ants are in their group?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 네 종류의 개미가 있다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: There are four kinds of ants in their group.

questions.question: Which ant lays eggs?
questions.evidence: The queen lays eggs.

questions.question: Who are the queen’s daughters?
questions.evidence: Workers are the queen’s daughters.

questions.question: What do workers gather?
questions.evidence: They build houses and gather food.

questions.question: What do soldiers defend?
questions.evidence: They defend houses and fight enemies.

questions.question: What do males do with the queen?
questions.explanationKo: 수개미는 여왕개미와 짝짓기하여 알을 낳게 합니다.
questions.evidence: Males mate with the queen to produce eggs.

questions.question: Which group member protects the ant house?
questions.explanationKo: 집을 지키고 적과 싸우는 개미는 병정개미입니다.
questions.evidence: They defend houses and fight enemies.

questions.question: Why is the queen important in the group?
questions.evidence: The queen lays eggs.

questions.question: What do workers do after building houses?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 일개미는 집을 짓고 먹이를 모은다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: They build houses and gather food.

passages.title: Art Class
passage: Today is art class.
Sam doesn't know what to draw.
"Draw anything you want," says Ms. Blair.
Sam thinks for a while.
He puts his right handprint on one side of the paper.
He puts his left handprint on the other side.
He adds two spots for eyes.
He adds more colors for wings.
"What a beautiful butterfly!" shouts Ms. Blair.

passages.title: Shapes Can Be Art
passage: Artists draw different pictures.
They draw flowers or animals.
They draw houses or people.
Some artists draw only shapes.
Look at the picture.
What do you see?
We only see rectangles.
But it is a wonderful work of art.
How about this picture?
Circles, rectangles, squares, and triangles!
We see different kinds of shapes.
Different shapes make wonderful art.
Draw shapes to make art.
You can be an artist, too.

questions.question: Which word means "to make a picture with a pencil or pen"?
Which word means "to use your mind to decide something"?
Which word means "on the side of the body used by most people"?
Which word means "the mark made by a hand"?
Which word means "a small round mark"?
Which word means "a person who makes art"?
Which word means "not the same"?
Which word means "the form of an object"?
Which word means "a shape with four straight sides"?
Which word means "a round shape"?
What class is it today?
questions.evidence: Today is art class.

questions.question: What does Sam not know?
questions.evidence: Sam doesn’t know what to draw.

questions.question: What does Ms. Blair tell Sam to draw?
questions.evidence: Draw anything you want

questions.question: Where does Sam put his right handprint?
choices: On one side of the paper
questions.explanationKo: Sam은 오른손 자국을 종이 한쪽에 찍습니다.
questions.evidence: He puts his right handprint on one side of the paper.
choiceMeanings: one side of 그 paper 위에/에서

questions.question: What does Sam add for eyes?
questions.evidence: He adds two spots for eyes.

questions.question: What does Sam add more colors for?
questions.evidence: He adds more colors for wings.

questions.question: What does Ms. Blair call the picture?
questions.evidence: What a beautiful butterfly!

questions.question: What is Sam trying to make with his handprints?
questions.explanationKo: 손자국과 점, 색을 더해 나비를 만듭니다.
questions.evidence: What a beautiful butterfly!

questions.question: What does Sam do after putting his handprints on paper?
choices: He adds two spots for eyes.
questions.explanationKo: 손자국을 찍은 다음 눈이 될 점을 더합니다.
questions.evidence: He adds two spots for eyes.
choiceMeanings: 그는 adds two 점들 for 눈

questions.question: What happens after Sam adds colors for wings?
choices: Ms. Blair says it is a beautiful butterfly.
questions.explanationKo: 색을 더한 뒤 선생님이 아름다운 나비라고 칭찬합니다.
questions.evidence: What a beautiful butterfly!
choiceMeanings: ms blair says it is 아름다운 butterfly

questions.question: What do artists draw?
questions.evidence: Artists draw different pictures.

questions.question: What can artists draw?
questions.explanationKo: 예술가들은 꽃이나 동물을 그릴 수 있습니다.
questions.evidence: They draw flowers or animals.

questions.question: What do some artists draw only?
questions.evidence: Some artists draw only shapes.

questions.question: What shape do we only see in one picture?
questions.evidence: We only see rectangles.

questions.question: What shapes are in the second picture?
questions.explanationKo: 두 번째 그림에는 여러 도형이 나옵니다.
questions.evidence: Circles, rectangles, squares, and triangles!

questions.question: What do different shapes make?
questions.evidence: Different shapes make wonderful art.

questions.question: Who can be an artist, too?
questions.explanationKo: 본문에서 너도 예술가가 될 수 있다고 했습니다.
questions.evidence: You can be an artist, too.

questions.question: Which can be used to make art?
questions.explanationKo: 본문의 핵심 개념은 도형도 예술이 될 수 있다는 것입니다.
questions.evidence: Different shapes make wonderful art.

questions.question: What does “You can be an artist, too” mean?
questions.explanationKo: 너도 예술을 만들 수 있다는 뜻입니다.
questions.evidence: You can be an artist, too.

questions.question: What happens when different shapes are put together?
questions.explanationKo: 여러 도형이 모이면 멋진 예술 작품이 됩니다.
questions.evidence: Different shapes make wonderful art.

passages.title: I'm a Giant Panda!
passage: Hi! My name is Bao Bao.
I live in a zoo in Washington, D.C.
I belong to the bear family.
Bears and I have many things in common.
We have sharp teeth and claws.
We like to climb trees.
But I'm different from bears.
I don't sleep in winter.
Because I don't eat meat, I don't hunt.
I eat a plant called bamboo.
Can you guess who I am?
I'm a giant panda!

passages.title: Why Pandas Are Black and White
passage: Long ago, pandas were white as snow.
One day, a leopard shows up.
It scares the little cubs.
A brave girl hears the cubs crying.
She saves the cubs.
But she is killed by the leopard.
All the pandas are sad and cry.
They wipe their eyes with their paws.
They hug each other.
There is dust on their paws.
So black marks are left around their eyes.
Over their fur, too.
Now the pandas are not white anymore.

questions.question: Which word means "to be a member of a group"?
Which word means "having the same features"?
questions.explanationKo: in common의 뜻을 묻는 영영정의형 문제입니다.
questions.hintKo: in common의 뜻을 묻는 영영정의형 문제입니다.

questions.question: Which word means "having a thin edge or point that can cut"?
Which word means "a sharp curved nail on an animal"?
Which word means "a tall tropical plant"?
Which word means "soft white pieces of frozen water"?
Which word means "a large spotted wild cat"?
Which word means "to make someone afraid"?
Which word means "a baby bear, lion, or fox"?
Which word means "to produce tears"?
What is the panda’s name?
questions.evidence: Hi! My name is Bao Bao.

questions.question: Where does Bao Bao live?
choices: In a zoo in Washington, D.C.
questions.explanationKo: Bao Bao는 워싱턴 D.C.의 동물원에 삽니다.

questions.evidence: I eat a plant called bamboo.

passages.title: Apples Are Good for You!
passage: There are different types of apples.
They are red, yellow, and green apples.
All types of apples taste great!
Apples are good for you.
They are very nutritious.
They keep your body healthy.
They help you grow well.
You can enjoy apples in many ways.
You can make apple juice or apple pie.
You can make apple jam or apple sauce.
You can also eat them raw.

passages.title: Johnny Appleseed
passage: There are many apple trees in America.
It's thanks to Johnny Appleseed.
Johnny was born in 1774.
His real name was John Chapman.
He was an apple farmer.
He grew apple trees.
He knew apples were very good.
He wanted to feed people apples.
So he traveled and planted apple trees.
He planted apple trees for 50 years.
He was simple and nice.
He wore a pot on his head as a hat.
Everyone liked him.
They called him Johnny Appleseed.

questions.question: Which word means "one member of a particular kind"?

questions.evidence: Johnny was born in 1774.

questions.evidence: His real name was John Chapman.

questions.evidence: He grew apple trees.

questions.evidence: He wanted to feed people apples.

choices: He traveled and planted apple trees.

choices: Because he lived in a hole.
questions.explanationKo: 사과나무를 심고 다녀서 Johnny Appleseed라고 불렸습니다.
questions.evidence: So he traveled and planted apple trees.

passages.title: Germs Are All Around Us!
passage: Germs are very tiny.
We can't see them.
But germs are all around us.
Germs can give you a stomachache.
Germs can give you a cold.
Germs grow bigger in wet places.
Germs grow stronger in the warm air.
Oh! You don't like germs.
Then, wash your hands.
Keep your food in the fridge.
Germs can't live in clean dry places.
Germs can't live in the cold fresh air.

passages.title: Protect Yourself From Germs!
passage: Are you a nose-picker?
Are you a finger-licker?
If you are, germs will attack you.
Do you feel tired?
Do you feel weak?
If you do, germs won't miss their chance.
Here are the ways to protect yourself from germs.
Wash hands with soap and water.
Keep your hands off your face.
Eat more fruits and vegetables.
Do exercises every day.
Get enough sleep at night.
Always stay healthy and strong.
Do not give germs a chance.

Which word means "needing rest or sleep"?

How big are germs?
questions.evidence: Germs are very tiny.

questions.question: Can we see germs?
questions.evidence: We can’t see them.

questions.question: Where are germs?
questions.evidence: But germs are all around us.

questions.evidence: Germs can give you a stomachache.

questions.evidence: Germs can give you a cold.

questions.evidence: Germs grow bigger in wet places.

questions.question: What should you do if you do not like germs?
questions.evidence: Then, wash your hands.

choices: Because germs can’t live in the cold fresh air.
questions.explanationKo: 차갑고 신선한 공기에서는 세균이 살 수 없기 때문입니다.
questions.evidence: Keep your food in the fridge. Germs can’t live in the cold fresh air.
choiceMeanings: 왜냐하면 세균은 can't live in 그 차가운 신선한 공기

questions.explanationKo: 세균은 깨끗하고 건조한 곳에서 살 수 없습니다.
questions.evidence: Germs can’t live in clean dry places.

choices: Germs can be everywhere near us.

questions.evidence: But germs are all around us.
choiceMeanings: 세균은 우리 주변 어디에나 있을 수 있다.

questions.evidence: If you are, germs will attack you.

questions.question: What happens if you feel weak?
questions.evidence: If you do, germs won’t miss their chance.

questions.question: What are the passage’s ways about?
questions.explanationKo: 본문은 세균으로부터 자신을 지키는 방법을 설명합니다.
questions.evidence: Here are the ways to protect yourself from germs.

questions.question: What should you wash hands with?
questions.evidence: Wash hands with soap and water.

questions.question: What should you keep off your face?
questions.evidence: Keep your hands off your face.

questions.question: What should you eat more?
questions.evidence: Eat more fruits and vegetables.

questions.question: What should you do every day?
questions.evidence: Do exercises every day.

questions.question: Which is one way to protect yourself after washing hands?
choices: Keep your hands off your face.
questions.explanationKo: 손 씻기 다음에 얼굴을 만지지 않는 방법이 제시됩니다.
questions.evidence: Wash hands with soap and water. Keep your hands off your face.

questions.question: Which habit helps stop germs?
questions.explanationKo: 충분한 수면은 건강을 지켜 세균에게 기회를 주지 않는 방법입니다.
questions.evidence: Get enough sleep at night.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: Stay healthy to protect yourself from germs.
questions.explanationKo: 전체 글은 건강하게 생활해 세균으로부터 자신을 지키자는 내용입니다.
questions.evidence: Always stay healthy and strong. Do not give germs a chance.

passages.title: Portraits
passage: Artists paint portraits to show how people look.
In this picture, there are two girls with their mother, and they look happy.
Some artists paint portraits to record history.
In the past, there were no cameras.
So rich people would ask artists to draw their portraits.
In this picture, there is a king wearing expensive clothes.
Sometimes artists also paint self-portraits.
This is Vincent van Gogh, a famous artist, and he painted himself.

passages.title: A Portrait of the One-Legged King
passage: "Do not make my portrait," says the king.
He is handsome and brave.
But he has only one leg.
He doesn’t want a portrait showing his one leg.
One day, a wise painter comes and says, "I’ll paint a beautiful portrait for you."
The king is curious and wants to see his portrait soon.
Finally, the painter shows the portrait.
It’s a side view of the king riding a horse.
It’s very beautiful.
The king is very happy.
